channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Magic: The Gathering Arena Developers Announce Official United Wizards of the Coast Worker's Union.
item.content:encoded:
The developers of Magic: The Gathering Arena have announced the formation of a union. The union, called the United Wizards of the Coast, has recently been filed with the CWA and now awaits recognition by company Wizards of the Coast.

The announcement of the United Wizards of the Coast union happened across multiple social media posts linking to an official web page. The first news post on this site states that, as of last week, the vast supermajority of eligible workers on Magic: The Gathering Arena have signed union cards and pledged their support for the union. The union was made in affiliation with the Communications Workers of America.

In addition, the post states the union sent a letter earlier this morning to Wizards of the Coast, formally requesting the company voluntarily recognize the union. The letter lays out the value of the union's formation: improving worker well-being, ensuring equity, and creating a healthy work environment.

The letter also lays out some issues for why the United Wizards of the Coast union is forming. These issues include layoff protections, remote work protections, establishing worker protections and guidelines around the use of AI, sustainable workloads, clear career progression, and sustained long-term stewardship of the Magic: The Gathering brand.

We call on Hasbro and Wizards of the Coast to voluntarily recognize our union and to publicly commit to remaining neutral during our unionization efforts—not just toward the MTG Arena team, but to all Hasbro employees and unionization efforts, present and future. We believe in building bridges with our peers on other teams, not divisions. Respecting neutrality ensures a fair and democratic process for everyone.

The issues expressed by the United Wizards of the Coast union does provide some insight into the work environment within Wizards of the Coast. The concerns of AI protection is warranted given Hasbro CEO Chris Cocks' push for its use alongside changing Dungeons & Dragons from a TTRPG experience into a digital live-service platform.

In addition, the union's worries about job security is especially valid since their work is a videogame adaptation of the collectible card game. And news of mass layoffs and closures, regardless of work quality or job sales, within the videogame industry has become an everyday occurrence.

Wizards of the Coast has until the end of office hours on May 1, 2026 to voluntarily recognize the United Wizards of the Coast union.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/magic-gathering-arena-developers-announce-official-united-wizards-of-coast-workers
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
